name: Cloudflare Origin Access Lockdown Pack
description: Reduces direct-origin exposure by enforcing edge-only ingress patterns for sensitive hostnames and paths. Detects/block requests with unexpected Host headers, direct-IP host access, and missing edge-identifying controls where applicable.
objective: Ensure origin-facing services only accept intended Cloudflare edge traffic patterns and approved integration exceptions.
risk_mitigated: Origin IP bypass of Cloudflare security controls
Host header abuse and routing override attempts
assumptions: Sensitive hostnames and path patterns are known and can be maintained in allowlists.
Operators can provide trusted integration source IPs or explicit bypass conditions.
Cloudflare custom rules are deployed at the relevant zone(s).
deployment_notes: Start all rules in log mode for 7-14 days before enforcing block actions.
Review Security Events for legitimate traffic lacking expected edge-identifying headers.
Introduce explicit skip/bypass conditions for trusted third-party integrations as needed.
After tuning, promote high-confidence rules to block while keeping lower-confidence heuristics in log/challenge.
Pair with network-level origin ACLs (cloud provider security groups, firewalls) for defense in depth.
tuning_notes: Populate allowed hostnames list with exact production hostnames; avoid broad wildcard patterns when possible.
If using custom health checks/monitors, add scoped exceptions by source IP and path.
For partner/API integrations that must bypass edge, require strict IP allowlisting and dedicated auth controls.
If legacy clients omit expected headers, create narrow exceptions (path + method + source constraints) rather than global disables.
Continuously compare blocked/logged events against known deployment automation and synthetic monitoring traffic.
monitoring_guidance: Track spikes in matches for direct-IP host header rule and unexpected host rule.
Alert on repeated origin-lockdown hits against admin/auth/private API paths.
Measure false positive rate by integration type and exception rule usage.
Confirm blocked events correlate with non-edge source patterns in upstream network logs.

rules.description: Log/block requests to sensitive paths when Host header is not in approved list.
Log/block likely direct-origin access attempts using IP-literal Host headers.
Log/block sensitive path requests missing expected Cloudflare edge-identifying header.
Template bypass condition for trusted integrations (keep disabled until explicitly configured).

terraform_example.description: Example Cloudflare custom ruleset deploying this pack in default log mode. Replace zone_id and host/path values before production.
terraform_example.snippet:
resource "cloudflare_ruleset" "origin_access_lockdown" {
  zone_id = var.zone_id
  name    = "Origin Access Lockdown Pack"
  kind    = "zone"
  phase   = "http_request_firewall_custom"

  rules {
    ref         = "cf-origin-lockdown-unexpected-host-sensitive-scope"
    description = "Unexpected host on sensitive paths"
    expression  = "(http.request.uri.path matches \"^/(admin|internal|private|auth|api/v[0-9]+/admin)(/.*)?$\") and not (lower(http.host) in {\"app.example.com\" \"api.example.com\" \"admin.example.com\"})"
    action      = "log"
    enabled     = true
  }

  rules {
    ref         = "cf-origin-lockdown-direct-ip-host-header"
    description = "Direct-IP or malformed Host header"
    expression  = "http.host matches \"^(?:\\d{1,3}\\.){3}\\d{1,3}$\" or http.host contains \":\""
    action      = "log"
    enabled     = true
  }

  rules {
    ref         = "cf-origin-lockdown-missing-cf-connecting-ip-sensitive"
    description = "Sensitive path missing CF-Connecting-IP"
    expression  = "(http.request.uri.path matches \"^/(admin|internal|private|auth)(/.*)?$\") and not any(lower(http.request.headers.names[*])[*] eq \"cf-connecting-ip\")"
    action      = "log"
    enabled     = true
  }
}
